Fixes #27344


#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR overrides the expectation method of SingleDiscreteDistribution in PoissonDistribution, which simply returns the correct expected value. 
A test has been added corresponding to this change.

Fixes #27344


#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR overrides the expectation method of SingleDiscreteDistribution in PoissonDistribution, which simply returns the correct expected value. 
A test has been added corresponding to this change.

Fixes #27344


#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR overrides the expectation method of SingleDiscreteDistribution in PoissonDistribution, which simply returns the correct expected value. 
A test has been added corresponding to this change.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR overrides the expectation method of SingleDiscreteDistribution in PoissonDistribution, which simply returns the correct expected value. 
A test has been added corresponding to this change.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR overrides the expectation method of SingleDiscreteDistribution in PoissonDistribution, which simply returns the correct expected value. 
A test has been added corresponding to this change.

Fixes #27344


#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR overrides the expectation method of SingleDiscreteDistribution in PoissonDistribution, which simply returns the correct expected value. 
A test has been added corresponding to this change.
#### Other comments

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR overrides the expectation method of SingleDiscreteDistribution in PoissonDistribution, which simply returns the correct expected value. 
A test has been added corresponding to this change.

Fixes #27344


#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR overrides the expectation method of SingleDiscreteDistribution in PoissonDistribution, which simply returns the correct expected value. 
A test has been added corresponding to this change.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR overrides the expectation method of SingleDiscreteDistribution in PoissonDistribution, which simply returns the correct expected value. 
A test has been added corresponding to this change.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27358","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27358/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27358/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27358/events","html_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/issues/27358","id":2730136784,"node_id":"I_kwDOAAnGFs6iupTQ","number":27358,"title":"Integrate piecewise 2D function","user":{"login":"gsutra","id":14281560,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MjgxNTYw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14281560?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gsutra","html_url":"https://github.com/gsutra","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gsutra/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gsutra/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gsutra/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gsutra/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gsutra/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gsutra/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gsutra/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gsutra/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gsutra/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-12-10T13:31:51Z","updated_at":"2024-12-10T16:23:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I try to compute the area of a 2D shape described by a piecewise function f(x,y).
After declaring the piecewise function, I call method `integrate` over a defined range of values for `x` and `y`.
Below is an example where the shape is a circle.

```python
    from sympy import symbols, integrate, Piecewise
    x, y = symbols('x y')
    xc, yc, rc = 5, 5, 1
    circle = (x - xc)**2 + (y - yc)**2 <= rc**2
    circle = Piecewise((1, circle), (0, True))
    integ = integrate(circle, (x, (0, 9)), (y, (0, 9)))
```

I expect the variable `integ` to be a float number (the shape area). But instead it is an instance of `sympy.Integral`.
Calling `evalf()` does not help.

Is it possible to get the result of the evaluated integral ?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27358/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27358/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/comments/2532205227","html_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/issues/27358#issuecomment-2532205227","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27358","id":2532205227,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAAnGFs6W7mKr","user":{"login":"RushabhMehta2005","id":139112780,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEqxTA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139112780?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005","html_url":"https://github.com/RushabhMehta2005","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-10T16:23:02Z","updated_at":"2024-12-10T16:23:02Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Whenever I get stuck with integrals I try a few things like .evalf() or .doit(). In this particular case:

```python

circle.integrate(x, y).doit() 

```
gives 

$$
\int 
\begin{cases} 
x & \text{for} \: \left(x - 5\right)^{2} + \left(y - 5\right)^{2} \leq 1 \\
0 & \text{otherwise} 
\end{cases}
\, dy
$$

whereas 
```python

circle.piecewise_integrate((x, y))

```

gives 

$$
\begin{cases} 
y & \text{for} \: \left(x - 5\right)^{2} + \left(y - 5\right)^{2} \leq 1 \\
0 & \text{otherwise} 
\end{cases}
$$

Not sure where to go from here though.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/comments/2532205227/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

write a comment on that issue linking back to this pull request once it is

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR implements multiplication operation of a transverse wave (TWave) with a symbolic scalar (Symbol) i.e amplitude scaling by a Symbol.
Example Usage:

```python
>>> A, f, phi= symbols('A, f, phi')
>>> s = symbols('s') # scaling factor
>>> w = TWave(A, f, phi)
>>> w2 = w*s
>>> w2
TWave(A*s, f, phi, 1/f, n)
```


Tests have also been added. 

#### Other comments
Any feedback and suggestions on improving/enhancing this implementation are appreciated.

#### Release Notes

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR implements multiplication operation of a transverse wave (TWave) with a symbolic scalar (Symbol) i.e amplitude scaling by a Symbol.
Example Usage:

```python
>>> A, f, phi= symbols('A, f, phi')
>>> s = symbols('s') # scaling factor
>>> w = TWave(A, f, phi)
>>> w2 = w*s
>>> w2
TWave(A*s, f, phi, 1/f, n)
```


Tests have also been added. 

#### Other comments
Any feedback and suggestions on improving/enhancing this implementation are appreciated.

#### Release Notes

<!-- Write the release notes for this release below between the BEGIN and END
statements. The basic format is a bulleted list with the name of the subpackage
and the release note for this PR. For example:

* solvers
  * Added a new solver for logarithmic equations.

* functions
  * Fixed a bug with log of integers. Formerly, `log(-x)` incorrectly gave `-log(x)`.

* physics.units
  * Corrected a semantical error in the conversion between volt and statvolt which
    reported the volt as being larger than the statvolt.

or if no release note(s) should be included use:

NO ENTRY

See https://github.com/sympy/sympy/wiki/Writing-Release-Notes for more

<!-- BEGIN RELEASE NOTES -->
* physics.optics
  * Symbolic amplitude scaling of waves is now supported.
<!-- END RELEASE NOTES -->

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR implements multiplication operation of a transverse wave (TWave) with a symbolic scalar (Symbol) i.e amplitude scaling by a Symbol.
Example Usage:

```python
>>> A, f, phi= symbols('A, f, phi')
>>> s = symbols('s') # scaling factor
>>> w = TWave(A, f, phi)
>>> w2 = w*s
>>> w2
TWave(A*s, f, phi, 1/f, n)
```


Tests have also been added.

Any feedback and suggestions on improving/enhancing this implementation are appreciated.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR implements multiplication operation of a transverse wave (TWave) with a symbolic scalar (Symbol) i.e amplitude scaling by a Symbol.
Example Usage:

```python
>>> A, f, phi= symbols('A, f, phi')
>>> s = symbols('s') # scaling factor
>>> w = TWave(A, f, phi)
>>> w2 = w*s
>>> w2
TWave(A*s, f, phi, 1/f, n)
```


Tests have also been added.

#### Other comments
Any feedback and suggestions on improving/enhancing this implementation are appreciated.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR implements multiplication operation of a transverse wave (TWave) with a symbolic scalar (Symbol) i.e amplitude scaling by a Symbol.
Example Usage:

```python
>>> A, f, phi= symbols('A, f, phi')
>>> s = symbols('s') # scaling factor
>>> w = TWave(A, f, phi)
>>> w2 = w*s
>>> w2
TWave(A*s, f, phi, 1/f, n)
```


Tests have also been added.

#### Other comments
Any feedback and suggestions on improving/enhancing this implementation are appreciated.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR implements multiplication operation of a transverse wave (TWave) with a symbolic scalar (Symbol) i.e amplitude scaling by a Symbol.
Example Usage:

```python
>>> A, f, phi= symbols('A, f, phi')
>>> s = symbols('s') # scaling factor
>>> w = TWave(A, f, phi)
>>> w2 = w*s
>>> w2
TWave(A*s, f, phi, 1/f, n)
```


Tests have also been added.

#### Other comments
Any feedback and suggestions on improving/enhancing this implementation are appreciated.

Fixes #27392

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

Minor Fix in `classify_ode`:

This PR addresses an issue in `classify_ode` where a constant `check1` was being differentiated instead of the intended unevaluated expression `check`. This oversight was initially identified by @anandkaranubc.  
The original code calculates the derivative *after* substituting specific values for `x` and `y`, turning the expression into a constant. 

#### Other comments
A regression test needs to be added still for this change.

Fixes #27392

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

Minor Fix in `classify_ode`:

This PR addresses an issue in `classify_ode` where a constant `check1` was being differentiated instead of the intended unevaluated expression `check`. This oversight was initially identified by @anandkaranubc.  
The original code calculates the derivative *after* substituting specific values for `x` and `y`, turning the expression into a constant. 

#### Other comments
A regression test needs to be added still for this change.

Your title above should be a short description of what
was changed. Do not include the issue number in the title. -->

#### References to other Issues or PRs
Fixes #27379

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Previously, the code incorrectly handled periodic functions like abs(cos(x)) when integration limits extended outside the interval
[0, T] (where T is the period). The issue was caused by splitting piecewise functions only within the domain [0, T]. As a result,
for limits outside this range, the integral evaluation returned incorrect results.

This commit fixes the issue by transforming the integration limits to (0, b) using the transform function.
The integration is then computed correctly using the formula:
∫(0 to b+nT) f(x) dx = ∫(0 to b) f(x) dx + n ∫(0 to T) f(x) dx.

**Key changes:**
- Added logic to detect periodicity using the `periodicity()` function.
- Transformed the integration limits for periodic functions to the range (0, b) to align with the formula.
- Properly handled integration direction and combined results for periodic segments and the remaining interval.

#### Other comments


#### Release Notes

<!-- If this pull request fixes an issue, write "Fixes #NNNN" in that exact

https://tinyurl.com/auto-closing for more information). Also, please
write a comment on that issue linking back to this pull request once it is
open. -->
Fixes #27392

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

Minor Fix in `classify_ode`:

This PR addresses an issue in `classify_ode` where a constant `check1` was being differentiated instead of the intended unevaluated expression `check`. This oversight was initially identified by @anandkaranubc.  
The original code calculates the derivative *after* substituting specific values for `x` and `y`, turning the expression into a constant. 

#### Other comments
A regression test needs to be added still for this change.

Fixes #27392

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

Minor Fix in `classify_ode`:

This PR addresses an issue in `classify_ode` where a constant `check1` was being differentiated instead of the intended unevaluated expression `check`. This oversight was initially identified by @anandkaranubc.  
The original code calculates the derivative *after* substituting specific values for `x` and `y`, turning the expression into a constant. 

#### Other comments
A regression test needs to be added still for this change.

* functions
  * Fixed a bug with log of integers. Formerly, `log(-x)` incorrectly gave `-log(x)`.

* physics.units
  * Corrected a semantical error in the conversion between volt and statvolt which
    reported the volt as being larger than the statvolt.

or if no release note(s) should be included use:

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR adds tests for the `integrate` method in `Quaternion` class in the `algebras` module. The only existing test called the integrate function instead of the class method.
A TODO in the method was also removed after verification of the expression.

#### Other comments
Any advice is most welcome. I am in the process of learning about sympy.

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

#### Other comments


#### Release Notes

  * Corrected a semantical error in the conversion between volt and statvolt which

* physics.wigner
  * Numpy floats can be used with wigner_3j

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

#### Other comments


#### Release Notes

<!-- Write the release notes for this release below between the BEGIN and END
statements. The basic format is a bulleted list with the name of the subpackage
and the release note for this PR. For example:

* solvers
  * Added a new solver for logarithmic equations.

* functions
  * Fixed a bug with log of integers. Formerly, `log(-x)` incorrectly gave `-log(x)`.

* physics.units
  * Corrected a semantical error in the conversion between volt and statvolt which
    reported the volt as being larger than the statvolt.

or if no release note(s) should be included use:

NO ENTRY

See https://github.com/sympy/sympy/wiki/Writing-Release-Notes for more
information on how to write release notes. The bot will check your release
notes automatically to see if they are formatted correctly. -->

<!-- BEGIN RELEASE NOTES -->
* physics.wigner
  * Numpy floats can be used with wigner_3j

<!-- END RELEASE NOTES Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

#### Other comments

write a comment on that issue linking back to this pull request once it is

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

#### Other comments


#### Release Notes

  * Fixed a bug with log of integers. Formerly, `log(-x)` incorrectly gave `-log(x)`.

* physics.units
  * Corrected a semantical error in the conversion between volt and statvolt which
    reported the volt as being larger than the statvolt.

or if no release note(s) should be included use:

NO ENTRY

* physics.wigner
  * Numpy floats can be used with wigner_3j

write a comment on that issue linking back to this pull request once it is

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

#### Other comments

  * Corrected a semantical error in the conversion between volt and statvolt which

  * Numpy floats can be used with wigner_3j

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

#### Other comments

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

#### Other comments

  * Numpy floats can be used with wigner_3j

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

#### Other comments

failure","user":{"login":"asterix314","id":10952409,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwOTUyNDA5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10952409?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/asterix314","html_url":"https://github.com/asterix314","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/asterix314/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/asterix314/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/asterix314/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/asterix314/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/asterix314/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/asterix314/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/asterix314/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/asterix314/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/asterix314/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":84627387,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw4NDYyNzM4Nw==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/labels/stats","name":"stats","color":"0000FF","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-10-30T07:32:48Z","updated_at":"2024-11-08T11:49:08Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"By both wikipedia and Wolfram mathworld, references given in the documentation page of `stats.NegativeBinomial(name, r, p)`, the `p` designates the probability of __success__. But in the current implementation and documentation, `p` seems to be the probability of failure, leading to surprising results to an unsuspecting user.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27203/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27203/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/comments/2464529133","html_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/issues/27203#issuecomment-2464529133","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27203","id":2464529133,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAAnGFs6S5brt","user":{"login":"RushabhMehta2005","id":139112780,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEqxTA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139112780?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005","html_url":"https://github.com/RushabhMehta2005","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RushabhMehta2005/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-08T11:49:07Z","updated_at":"2024-11-08T11:49:07Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"On Wikipedia, the probability mass function (PMF) for the negative binomial distribution is given as 

$\binom{k + r - 1}{k} (1 - p)^k p^r$


where `p` represents the probability of success.

However, in the implementation of the `NegativeBinomialDistribution` class in `stats/drv_types.py`, the formula used is 

$\binom{k + r - 1}{k} (1 - p)^r p^k$

which suggests that `p` is being treated as the probability of failure. This results in `p` and `1 - p` being swapped compared to the wikipedia page.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/comments/2464529133/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

#### Other comments

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

#### Other comments

  * Numpy floats can be used with wigner_3j

write a comment on that issue linking back to this pull request once it is

Fixes #27219 

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_3j function to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This solves the issue where it previously raised a ValueError when encountered with a numpy float.

#### Other comments

  * Corrected a semantical error in the conversion between volt and statvolt which

* physics.wigner
  * Numpy floats can be used with wigner_3j

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR refactors the code where multiple sympify calls were made individually on the j and m values in the clebsch_gordan function. I used the map function to apply the sympify function on all the variables in a more concise and readable manner.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR refactors the code where multiple sympify calls were made individually on the j and m values in the clebsch_gordan function. I used the map function to apply the sympify function on all the variables in a more concise and readable manner.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27271","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27271/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27271/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27271/events","html_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/issues/27271","id":2669602604,"node_id":"I_kwDOAAnGFs6fHucs","number":27271,"title":"Library lacks direct square free querying functionality","user":{"login":"GregoryMorse","id":5968641,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU5Njg2NDE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5968641?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/GregoryMorse","html_url":"https://github.com/GregoryMorse","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GregoryMorse/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GregoryMorse/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GregoryMorse/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GregoryMorse/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GregoryMorse/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GregoryMorse/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GregoryMorse/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GregoryMorse/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GregoryMorse/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":84627349,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw4NDYyNzM0OQ==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/labels/ntheory","name":"ntheory","color":"0000FF","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-11-18T19:17:22Z","updated_at":"2024-11-19T11:04:30Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I would propose as it would enhance the library to add to `sympy.ntheory` possibly under `factor` an `is_square_free(t=2)` function that more efficiently computes the t-th square-freeness of a number.

Currently the following idioms can do this but in a non-efficient manner as this can be short-circuited:
1. `all(x<t for _, x in sympy.ntheory.factorint(n).items())`
2. `sympy.ntheory.factor_.core(n, t)==n`

I am confused about its expected behaviour on invalid inputs. Do I raise a ValueError for n <= 0 and t < 2? 

```python
from sympy.ntheory import factorint

def is_square_free(n, t=2):
    r"""
    Checks if a given integer is t-th square-free.

    A positive integer `n` is said to be t-th square-free if no prime factor 
    of `n` appears with an exponent greater than or equal to `t`. By default, 
    this function checks for square-freeness (t=2), meaning no prime factor 
    appears with an exponent of 2 or greater.

    Parameters
    ==========

    n : int
        A positive integer to be checked for t-th square-freeness.
    t : int, optional
        The value of `t` for t-th square-freeness. Defaults to 2.

    Returns
    =======

    bool
        True if `n` is t-th square-free, False otherwise.

    Examples
    ========

    >>> from sympy.ntheory.factor_ import is_square_free
    >>> is_square_free(10)  # 10 = 2^1 * 5^1 (t=2)
    True
    >>> is_square_free(12)  # 12 = 2^2 * 3^1 (t=2)
    False
    >>> is_square_free(18, t=3)  # 18 = 2^1 * 3^2 (t=3)
    True
    >>> is_square_free(16, t=3)  # 16 = 2^4 (t=3)
    False

    Notes
    =====

    - `n` must be a positive integer; otherwise, a `ValueError` is raised.
    - If `t < 2`, a `ValueError` is raised as t-th square-freeness is only 
      meaningful for `t >= 2`.
    - For `n = 1`, the function always returns True, as 1 is t-th square-free
      for all values of `t`.
    """

    if n <= 0:
        raise ValueError("Only positive integers can be checked for t-th square-freeness.")
    if n == 1:
        return True
    if t < 2:
        raise ValueError("t must be at least 2 for meaningful t-th square-freeness.")

    factors = factorint(n)

    # Check if any prime factor has an exponent >= t
    for exponent in factors.values():
        if exponent >= t:
            return False

    return True

# Usage
print(is_square_free(10, 2))  # True, 10 = 2^1 * 5^1
print(is_square_free(12, 2))  # False, 12 = 2^2 * 3^1 
print(is_square_free(72, 3))  # False, 72 = 2^3 * 3^2
print(is_square_free(72, 4))  # True, 72 = 2^3 * 3^2
print(is_square_free(18, 3))  # True, 18 = 2 * 3^2
print(is_square_free(16, 3))  # False, 16 = 2^4

Fixes #27271

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR adds 4 new functions to the ntheory module in Sympy.
- Added `is_kth_power_free` to check for k-th power-freeness of a number, similarly `is_square_free` is added for special case of k = 2.
- Added `kth_power_free_range` to generate a list of k-th power free numbers in a given range, similarly for `square_free_range`.
- Added tests and docstrings for all 4 functions

#### Other comments


#### Release Notes

    reported the volt as being larger than the statvolt.

Fixes #27285

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_6j and wigner_9j functions to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This now allows them to accept numpy floats.

#### Other comments

  * Corrected a semantical error in the conversion between volt and statvolt which

  * Numpy floats can be used with wigner_6j and wigner_9j functions

Fixes #27285

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_6j and wigner_9j functions to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This now allows them to accept numpy floats.
Also added tests for this behaviour.

#### Other comments


#### Release Notes

<!-- Write the release notes for this release below between the BEGIN and END
statements. The basic format is a bulleted list with the name of the subpackage

  * Added a new solver for logarithmic equations.

* functions
  * Fixed a bug with log of integers. Formerly, `log(-x)` incorrectly gave `-log(x)`.

* physics.units
  * Corrected a semantical error in the conversion between volt and statvolt which
    reported the volt as being larger than the statvolt.

or if no release note(s) should be included use:

NO ENTRY

See https://github.com/sympy/sympy/wiki/Writing-Release-Notes for more
information on how to write release notes. The bot will check your release
notes automatically to see if they are formatted correctly. -->

<!-- BEGIN RELEASE NOTES -->
* physics.wigner
  * Numpy floats can be used with wigner_6j and wigner_9j functions
<!-- END RELEASE NOTES -->

write a comment on that issue linking back to this pull request once it is

Fixes #27285

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_6j and wigner_9j functions to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This now allows them to accept numpy floats.
Also added tests for this behaviour.

#### Other comments

  * Corrected a semantical error in the conversion between volt and statvolt which

  * Numpy floats can be used with wigner_6j and wigner_9j functions

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

Also added tests for this behaviour.

Fixes #27271

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR adds 4 new functions to the ntheory module in Sympy.
- Added `is_kth_power_free` to check for k-th power-freeness of a number, similarly `is_square_free` is added for special case of k = 2.
- Added `kth_power_free_range` to generate a list of k-th power free numbers in a given range, similarly for `square_free_range`.
- Added tests and docstrings for all 4 functions

#### Other comments

#### References to other Issues or PRs

https://tinyurl.com/auto-closing for more information). Also, please
write a comment on that issue linking back to this pull request once it is
open. -->
Fixes #27285

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_6j and wigner_9j functions to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This now allows them to accept numpy floats.
Also added tests for this behaviour.

#### Other comments


#### Release Notes

<!-- Write the release notes for this release below between the BEGIN and END

* functions
  * Fixed a bug with log of integers. Formerly, `log(-x)` incorrectly gave `-log(x)`.

* physics.units
  * Corrected a semantical error in the conversion between volt and statvolt which
    reported the volt as being larger than the statvolt.

or if no release note(s) should be included use:

NO ENTRY

See https://github.com/sympy/sympy/wiki/Writing-Release-Notes for more
information on how to write release notes. The bot will check your release
notes automatically to see if they are formatted correctly. -->

<!-- BEGIN RELEASE NOTES -->
* physics.wigner
  * Numpy floats can be used with wigner_6j and wigner_9j functions

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
Modified the wigner_6j and wigner_9j functions to ensure compatibility with numpy floats by adding calls to sympify for the input params. This now allows them to accept numpy floats.
Also added tests for this behaviour.

Fixes #27271

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR adds 4 new functions to the ntheory module in Sympy.
- Added `is_kth_power_free` to check for k-th power-freeness of a number, similarly `is_square_free` is added for special case of k = 2.
- Added `kth_power_free_range` to generate a list of k-th power free numbers in a given range, similarly for `square_free_range`.
- Added tests and docstrings for all 4 functions

#### Other comments

How might we solve this ?
Is there a function that maybe decomposes n into p and q where n = pq ?

Fixes #27271

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed
This PR adds 4 new functions to the ntheory module in Sympy.
- Added `is_kth_power_free` to check for k-th power-freeness of a number, similarly `is_square_free` is added for special case of k = 2.
- Added `kth_power_free_range` to generate a list of k-th power free numbers in a given range, similarly for `square_free_range`.
- Added tests and docstrings for all 4 functions

The inner integral is computed correctly as expected; however, SymPy is unable to evaluate the rest. The issue appears to stem from the fact that the `trigintegrate` function only matches the pattern \( \sin(x)^n \cdot \cos(x)^m \), which our function \( \sqrt{4 - 4 \cos^2(x)} \) does not match. Despite this, within the given bounds (0, \( \frac{\pi}{2} \)), it simplifies to \( 2 \sin(x) \).

Is there a way to convert $`\sqrt{4 - 4 \cos^2(x)} \`$ to this simpler form so that it can be properly handled by the `trigintegrate` function? 

Thank you for your assistance!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/comments/2444861044/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
